summary: Postinstall credential harvester exfiltrates npm/AWS/git tokens to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` hook (`node postinstall.js`) reads installer-side credential files (`~/.npmrc`, `~/.git-credentials`, `~/.env`, `./.env`, `~/.bash_history`) and scans them with regexes tuned for npm publish tokens (`npm_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}`, `_authToken`, `NPM_TOKEN`), AWS access keys (`AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`), and git credentials. Collected secrets, together with `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo()`, are JSON-encoded and POSTed in cleartext over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP command-and-control endpoint `http://149.28.127.35:8888`. The script file comment self-identifies as an 'npm token harvester' and the transmission is deliberately silent ('No console.log — zero trace.'). The package's visible surface is a decoy: `index.js` is an empty shim that comments itself as 'lodash-js — Just a dummy module. The real payload is in postinstall.js', and the package is published under the name `glob-helper` with `lodash` keywords, a namespace-squat cover story. Harvested npm tokens enable downstream package-takeover / self-propagation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
